title: dactylorhiza sulphurea subsp. pseudosambucina
intro: Dactylorhiza sulphurea subsp. pseudosambucina, also known as the golden-rimmed marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid that is native to certain regions in Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, and its herbal preparations have been used to treat digestive issues and promote wound healing. Dactylorhiza sulphurea subsp. pseudosambucina has been traditionally prepared as a tincture or tea, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
